raw_text:
ERROR   [TC-C-003] interface.f90:5 <-> header.h:2  param[0] 'buf'
  POINTER_DEPTH: Fortran TYPE(c_ptr) without VALUE passes void** (pointer
  to pointer). C prototype declares void* (single pointer). Callee writes
  to the address stored in 'buf', which C sees as the pointed-to data.
  Allocator writes to wrong location; likely heap corruption.

NOTE: Fix: add VALUE to Fortran 'buf' if C expects void*, or change
      C prototype to void **buf.
